# Let's try to read the text file(s) to get exact content and compute spans.
import os, re, json, textwrap, sys, codecs, unicodedata, pathlib

paths_to_try = [
    "/mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt",
    "/mnt/data/file-BnfuA2w77pgVLXtumjGdKK-Q108.txt",
]

content = None
used_path = None
for p in paths_to_try:
    if os.path.exists(p):
        with open(p, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            content = f.read()
        used_path = p
        break

used_path, content[:2000] if content else "NOT FOUND"[:1000]

# Let's display the last 500 characters and also the repr to see exact characters
print(content[-500:])
print("---- REPR ----")
print(repr(content[-500:]))

# Let's parse the content to find blockquote citations and compute spans

def find_blockquote_citations(text):
    citations = []
    pos = 0
    lines = text.splitlines(keepends=True)  # keep newlines to maintain positions
    for ln in lines:
        stripped = ln.lstrip(' \t')
        # Check if the line starts with '>' after optional spaces/tabs
        if stripped.startswith('>'):
            # find index of '>' in this line and compute base
            idx_gt = ln.find('>')
            # Search for the first double quote after '>'
            idx_quote1 = ln.find('"', idx_gt+1)
            if idx_quote1 != -1:
                # Try to find closing double quote after it
                idx_quote2 = ln.find('"', idx_quote1+1)
                if idx_quote2 != -1:
                    # Regular case - text between the quotes
                    span_start = pos + idx_quote1 + 1
                    span_end = pos + idx_quote2
                    span_text = text[span_start:span_end]
                    citations.append((span_start, span_end, span_text))
                else:
                    # No closing double quote; take until end-of-line
                    # Exclude a trailing single quote if present just before newline
                    # Identify the end index of the line excluding newline
                    ln_no_newline = ln[:-1] if ln.endswith('\n') else ln
                    trailing_single_quote = ln_no_newline.endswith("'")
                    end_in_line = len(ln_no_newline) - (1 if trailing_single_quote else 0)
                    span_start = pos + idx_quote1 + 1
                    span_end = pos + end_in_line
                    span_text = text[span_start:span_end]
                    citations.append((span_start, span_end, span_text))
            else:
                # no quote found - skip
                pass
        pos += len(ln)
    return citations

cits = find_blockquote_citations(content)
cits, [content[s:e] for s,e,_ in cits]

# Build the JSON object according to the schema
citations_list = []
# From previous extraction:
extracted = [
    {
        "span_start": 375,
        "span_end": 440,
        "span_text": 'وَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ جَعَلْتَ لَهُ قُرُونًا مُّتَرَتِيبَةً',
        "citation_type": "Aya"
    },
    {
        "span_start": 573,
        "span_end": 672,
        "span_text": 'فَإِنَّ الْأَمْرَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَكُمْ إِلَّا الْخَيْرُ أَوْ لَمْ تَكُنْ فِي الْقُرْآنِ مِنْهُ رِجْسٌ',
        "citation_type": "Aya"
    },
    {
        "span_start": 787,
        "span_end": 858,
        "span_text": 'فَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا وَلَا تَسْتَغْلِبُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُسْتَغْلِبِينَ',
        "citation_type": "Aya"
    },
    {
        "span_start": 1101,
        "span_end": 1150,
        "span_text": 'مَنْ قَرَأَ حَرْفًا مِنْ كِتَابِ اللَّهِ فَلَهُ أ',
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth"
    }
]

for item in extracted:
    base = {
        "span_start": item["span_start"],
        "span_end": item["span_end"],
        "span_text": item["span_text"],
        "citation_type": item["citation_type"],
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {} if item["citation_type"] == "Aya" else {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {} if item["citation_type"] == "Hadeeth" else {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    }
    citations_list.append(base)

result_obj = {"citations": citations_list}
print(json.dumps(result_obj, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
